result:
## Summary

The issue was that users couldn't open CSV files in AnkiDroid when tapping on them from a file manager or other app.

### Root Cause

The standard MIME type `text/csv` was missing from two critical locations:

1. **`IntentHandler.kt`** (`getLaunchType()` at line 336-338): When an external app sends a CSV file to AnkiDroid, the intent's MIME type is resolved. The code only checked for `text/tab-separated-values` and `text/comma-separated-values`, but not `text/csv`. Android's `MimeTypeMap` returns `text/csv` for `.csv` files, and many file managers use this standard MIME type. Files with `text/csv` MIME type fell through to `FILE_IMPORT` (the APKG/COLPKG handler), which rejected them because they aren't valid deck packages.

2. **`AndroidManifest.xml`** (line 244): The MIME-type-based intent filter didn't include `text/csv`, so Android wouldn't even suggest AnkiDroid as an app to open `text/csv` files when the file extension isn't visible to the intent filter (e.g., from email clients like Gmail that hide file extensions).

### Changes Made (2 files)

- **`AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/IntentHandler.kt`**: Added `mimeType == "text/csv"` to the `TEXT_IMPORT` detection condition in `getLaunchType()`
- **`AnkiDroid/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml`**: Added `<data android:mimeType="text/csv"/>` to the MIME-type-based intent filter

Note that `ImportFileSelectionFragment.kt` already included `text/csv` in its `extraMimes` array for the in-app file picker - the bug was only in the external intent handling path.
